ticket.subject: New customer message on 5 May 2026 at 23:41
ticket.customerBody:
You received a new message from your online store's contact form.

Country Code:
DE

Name:
Lukas Juelshøj

Email:
Lukasbj2002@icloud.com

Company / Team:

Your Country:
Danmark

If Applicable, Place Of Purchase And Order Number:
Date 26-04-2026 country Denmark Order #4350

What Is Your Request Regarding?:
A-Spire Wireless

What Do You Need Help With?:
Other

Body:
Dear AceZone Support,

I am writing to request a return and refund for my recently
purchased A-Spire Wireless headset (Order #4350).

After testing the product, I have decided that it does not meet
my expectations. Specifically, I am not satisfied with the
overall feel and comfort of the headset. At times, it feels like
there are pressure points or “spikes,” which makes it
uncomfortable to wear for longer sessions. Additionally, I have
experienced that the battery seems to drain relatively quickly.

I am also not fully satisfied with the microphone quality, as it
does not consistently perform at the level I expected.

Based on these factors, I would like to proceed with a return and
refund in accordance with your 30-day return policy. Please let
me know the next steps and confirm whether my return request is
approved.

Thank you for your assistance.

Kind regards,
Lukas

results.draft:
Hi Lukas,

Thanks for contacting us. I’m sorry to hear the A-Spire Wireless isn’t comfortable and that you’re seeing faster battery drain and inconsistent microphone quality.

Before we approve a return, please try these troubleshooting steps designed for the A-Spire Wireless. They address comfort‑related audio/mic issues and battery behaviour and help us confirm whether this is a product fault. Follow all steps below in order:

1) Update firmware via the AceZone Firmware Updater app on Windows
- Disconnect the USB‑C dongle from your PC.
- Connect the headset to your PC with the USB‑C cable and open the Firmware Updater app from the Microsoft Store.
- Select A‑Spire Wireless from the dropdown and click Update. After the headset update completes, select the dongle and click Update.

2) Uninstall/reinstall USB drivers (Windows)
- With the headset powered on and connected via USB‑C, open Device Manager.
- Under Sound, video and game controllers find the headset, right‑click and choose Uninstall driver.
- Unplug the headset, then plug it back into the same USB port so Windows reinstalls the driver.

3) Factory reset the headset and re‑pair with the dongle
- Turn the headset off. Press and hold the power button for 15 seconds until a voice prompt confirms the factory reset.
- Remove any saved pairings from your phone/PC and from the AceZone app.
- Plug the dongle into your PC. Reset the dongle by holding its button until the LED turns purple, then remove the dongle.
- Turn the headset on and ensure no cables are connected. Hold the Noise Cancelling mode button for 4 seconds to enter pairing mode.
- Plug the dongle back in. The LED will blink blue/red and pairing will happen automatically. The LED turns blue when done.

If these steps do not resolve the comfort, battery or microphone issues, we will proceed with a refund/return review for order #4350. At that point we will provide the return instructions and the next steps.

If you prefer to skip troubleshooting and proceed directly with a return now, tell us here in this thread and we will start the return process for you.

I look forward to your reply.

missing_for_10: confirmation of return approval and next steps
qa.reasoning: The response does not directly approve the return request or provide the next steps for the return process, which is essential for the customer's request.

ticket.subject: Re: [AceZone] Re: New customer message on 2 May 2026 at 03.17
ticket.customerBody:
Or could it be time to get a new headset after 2 years? I feel like  the
sound got a bit worse vs when I got it 2 years ago.  🫠

Den tirs. 5. maj 2026 kl. 20.29 skrev Simon Dec <simondec2021@gmail.com>:

results.draft:
Hi,

Thanks for contacting us, sorry to hear the sound feels worse after two years. We can help figure out whether it's age-related or something we can fix.

Please try these quick checks first:
1. Disable Windows sound enhancements: Press Win+R, type mmsys.cpl and Enter. In Playback, select the headset, right-click Properties, go to Enhancements and click Disable all enhancements. Then open Spatial sound and set it to Off.
2. Check balance/system settings: In the Playback device properties, open the Levels tab and click Balance to ensure left and right are equal.
3. If your headset has a factory reset procedure, perform a factory reset (this often resolves gradual issues caused by settings). If you already did a factory reset, skip this step.

If these steps do not restore the sound quality, this usually indicates a hardware issue after extended use and we can proceed with a warranty/quality review. If that is needed, we will start a warranty review for your headset.

If you want us to go ahead with a warranty review now instead of trying the steps above, say so and we will proceed.

If these steps don't resolve the issue, we'll go ahead with a warranty review.

I look forward to hearing from you.

missing_for_10: commit to warranty review and provide tracking link
qa.reasoning: The response lacks a clear commitment to initiate a warranty review and provide a tracking link, which is essential for customer assurance.

ticket.subject: Re: [AceZone] Re: New customer message on 4 May 2026 at 12:21
ticket.customerBody:
HI,

I applied this fix but unfortunately, I just had another instance where it disconnects and reconnects mid game again. Are there any other fixes I could possibly try?

Best regards,
Nathan
________________________________
Van: AceZone Support (Support) <support@acezone.io>
Verzonden: maandag 4 mei 2026 12:29
Aan: Nathan Segers <nathan.segers@hotmail.com>
Onderwerp: [AceZone] Re: New customer message on 4 May 2026 at 12:21

Hi Nathan,

Thank you for reaching out.

It seems that something may be interfering with your connection.
Could you inform me of how you have the dongle set up? - Is it placed at the back or top of your PC, and how far away are you sitting?

I recommend doing a factory reset of the headset + resetting dongle:

To do a factory reset on the A-Blaze:


  1.  Turn the headset off.
  2.  Press and hold power button for 15 seconds.
  3.  A voice prompt will tell you a factory reset has been made.


Please note that this will delete all saved Bluetooth connections from your headset, including your phone/PC and dongle. You should also delete the headset from your phone/PC's saved devices, as well as delete it from the AceZone app before pairing again. We'll need to re-pair the headset and the dongle:


  1.  Plug the dongle into your PC.
  2.  Reset the dongle by holding down the button until the LED turns purple.
  3.  Remove the dongle.
  4.  Turn your headset on, and make sure no cables are connected.
  5.  Hold the Noise Cancelling mode button (right next to the power button) for 4 seconds to enable pairing mode.
  6.  Plug the dongle back in. The LED will blink blue/red.
  7.  Pairing will happen automatically. The LED will turn blue when the pairing is done.


Make sure that when you are using dongle connection, try to disable bluetooth on your mobile device if you have had it connected previously.
Furthermore, please open your Mobile App and disable Standby Timer under System Settings 🙂

Let me know if any of these methods worked out!

Have a lovely day.

________________________________
Venlig Hilsen / Kind Regards,

[AceZone Logo]
Elias Knudsen
Support Associate
Acezone.io<http://acezone.io/>

This email is a service from AceZone. Delivered by Zendesk<https://www.zendesk.com/support/?utm_campaign=text&utm_content=AceZone&utm_medium=poweredbyzendesk&utm_source=email-notification>

results.draft:
Hi Nathan,

Thanks for the update, sorry to hear the disconnects continue. Don’t repeat the steps you already tried; next things to try and checks we’ll do now:

1) USB port and driver reset on PC
- With headset connected by USB-C, open Device Manager → Sound, video and game controllers → uninstall the headset drivers. Unplug the headset, then plug it back into the same USB port to reinstall drivers. If you switch ports later, repeat this process for that port.

2) Firmware check and update
- Use the AceZone Firmware Updater (Microsoft Store). Connect the headset via USB-C (dongle disconnected), open the app, select A-Blaze and update. After headset update, connect and update the dongle in the app as well.

3) Re-do factory reset + dongle re-pair (complete sequence)
- Turn headset off. Press and hold power button for 15 seconds until voice prompt confirms reset. Delete the headset from your PC and AceZone app saved devices. Plug dongle into PC, hold the dongle button until LED turns purple to reset, then remove dongle. Turn headset on, hold the Noise Cancelling mode button for 4 seconds to enter pairing mode. Plug dongle back in, LED will blink blue/red and then turn solid blue when paired.

4) Interference and placement checks
- Move the dongle to a front/top USB port or use a short USB extension so the dongle has clear line-of-sight to the headset. Keep other wireless devices (phones, Wi‑Fi dongles, other headsets) away from the dongle and headset while testing.

5) PC audio settings
- Ensure Windows sample rate for the headset mic is 48000 Hz. If you use USB audio, test whether the headset stays stable when used as a wired USB device; if wired is stable but dongle still drops, that further points to interference/USB issues.

If you’ve already done any of the above (drivers, firmware, full factory reset + dongle reset, placement change and PC settings), tell me which ones so I don’t repeat them. If these steps don’t stop the disconnects, we’ll go ahead with a warranty review.

I look forward to hearing which of the above you’ve tried and the result.

qa.reasoning: The response is thorough and accurate but could benefit from a slightly more empathetic tone to enhance customer experience.

Hey!

I have finally received my headset. But the second I put it on my head and
turned it on I heard a constant sort of rumbling sound, almost like someone
is blowing air in my left ear. It's definitely different from before I sent
it, but equally as distracting, and in the same ear.

Is this something you're aware of, and is there anything I'm doing wrong?
The sound persists regardless of whether it's connected to my PC by cable,
bluetooth, or not connected at all. It rises in volume for a few seconds,
then gradually goes down to a low rumble, before rising again.

I didn't have this issue when I first got the headset, and not with the
stand-in headset I loaned.

If you could be so kind as to give me a list of things to check, I'll get
straight to it.

Best regards,
Simen Valhaug.

On Fri, 1 May 2026, 09:09 AceZone Support (Support), <support@acezone.io>
wrote:

results.draft:
Hi Simen,

Thank you for contacting us. I’m sorry to hear you’re experiencing that rumbling sound.

Please try the following checks in order and report the results so we can proceed:

1. Factory reset the headset: power off, then press and hold the power button for 15 seconds until you hear the factory reset voice prompt. After reset, test again.
2. Test with no connections: power the headset on while not connected to any device and listen for the noise pattern you described.
3. Test wired vs wireless: connect by cable and check, then connect by Bluetooth and check. Note whether the behaviour is identical in all modes.
4. Check system audio balance on your PC/phone: ensure left/right balance is centered in system sound settings.
5. Disable any audio-enhancement software on your PC (eg voice-improvement or DSP apps) and ensure the headset is selected as the only output device.
6. Try another device (a different phone or PC) if you haven’t already, and confirm whether the issue follows the headset.

If these steps do not resolve the issue, this usually indicates a faulty headset and we will proceed with a warranty review for swap. If the troubleshooting does not resolve it, we will go ahead with a warranty review.

I look forward to your update on the test results.

missing_for_10: commit to warranty review and next steps
qa.reasoning: The response is mostly complete but lacks a clear commitment to initiate a warranty review if troubleshooting fails, which is essential for customer assurance.

results.draft:
Hello,

Thanks for the update, we acknowledge the pre-advise. Your shipment with tracking number 1Z4X4F259199813345 is registered as dispatched from Justin Megsmith to AceZone International ApS. You can track its progress using the carrier tracking number you provided. Tracking may take some time to appear until the carrier scans the parcel into their network.

If you need us to confirm receipt once it arrives, please let us know here in this thread. I look forward to hearing from you.

qa.reasoning: While the response is accurate and polite, it lacks the specific delivery date/time that would enhance the completeness and actionability of the message.
